message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add custom filters support to prompt decorator, only touches outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature need? If it also needs prompts.py we should coordinate closely or split by function.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) to outlines/prompts.py for template reusability. Let me know if your feature also needs this file so we can split functions/sections.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Same file, unavoidable since both features live in outlines/prompts.py per our task specs. Let's keep changes to distinct regions: I'll add a filters param to prompt()/Prompt.from_str()/Prompt.from_file(), plus a new create_jinja_env() helper that centralizes env creation+filter registration (built-in filters: name/description/source/signature/schema/args, plus any custom filters dict passed in). If your global registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) also needs to feed into env creation, feel free to have create_jinja_env() consult your registry too - just let me know the registry's variable/function name so I can wire it in, or we can merge and reconcile after. I'll proceed with implementation now and message you when DONE.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Same file, overlapping/dependent features (my global registry needs your local filters to exist so it can defer to them). Propose: you implement local 'filters' param (on prompt()/Prompt, applied to env.filters at template-creation or call time) first since mine builds on it. I'll add module-level _FILTERS dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter(), and merge global filters into env.filters BEFORE your local filters are applied so local ones win. Can you share the exact signature/placement you're planning (e.g. does prompt() gain a filters kwarg, and where do you call env.filters.update)? I'll implement against that and we'll merge via git at the end, resolving any line conflicts manually.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. Registry names: module-level dict outlines.prompts._FILTERS (Dict[str, Callable]), functions register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) and unregister_filter(name: str). If your create_jinja_env() accepts a filters dict, feel free to do env.filters.update(_FILTERS) first then env.filters.update(local_filters) so local overrides global - or I'll wire that merge myself after you push if easier. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Exact plan: module-level function create_jinja_env(filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment placed right after imports (before Prompt class). Body: build jinja2.Environment(trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, keep_trailing_newline=True, undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined), register built-ins env.filters['name'/'description'/'source'/'signature'/'schema'/'args'] = existing get_fn_* functions, then as the LAST step: 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)'. Prompt._template_from_str(cls, content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(cls, path, filters=None) both call create_jinja_env(filters) (file version additionally sets loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(dir) on the Environment call). Prompt.from_str(cls, content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(cls, path, filters=None) pass filters through. prompt() decorator becomes prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) supporting both bare @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an inner decorator(fn) closure. For your global registry: insert your merge line (e.g. env.filters.update(_FILTERS)) inside create_jinja_env AFTER the built-in name/description/source/signature/schema/args lines but BEFORE the final 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' line, so local filters passed to prompt()/from_str()/from_file() win over global registry ones, which win over built-ins. Starting implementation now - will ping DONE, then you can add your registry lines on top.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented: create_jinja_env(filters=None) module-level function (right after imports, before Prompt class); Prompt._template_from_str/_template_from_file now take filters=None and call create_jinja_env(filters); Prompt.from_str/from_file take filters=None and pass through; prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) decorator supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}). All existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass (13 passed). create_jinja_env body: builds Environment, sets built-in filters name/description/source/signature/schema/args, then 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' as the last step. Feel free to insert your env.filters.update(_FILTERS) line between the built-ins and that final if-block. Not committing yet - will wait for your DONE, then merge and resolve conflicts together.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My side is done and committed (register_filter/unregister_filter + _FILTERS dict, wired into the old _template_from_str/_template_from_file). Could you commit and push your branch now? I'll fetch, merge, and rebase my env.filters.update(_FILTERS) line into your new create_jinja_env() between the built-ins and the final local-filters update, then re-test and push the merged result.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Pushed my commit to team/agent1 (create_jinja_env + filters param on prompt()/from_str()/from_file()). Go ahead and merge/rebase your _FILTERS wiring on top. I'll independently merge team/agent2 into my branch too and reconcile, then push final. Ping me when your merged version is pushed.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2, resolved conflicts by using your create_jinja_env() and inserting env.filters.update(_FILTERS) between built-ins and the local filters override. All 13 tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass, and manual check confirms local filters override global registry filters override built-ins. Pushing now.
